description: Decision Matrix 1 of 8 in the NEPA CE Screener BRE. Routes incoming IndividualApplication records to CE-Recommended, EA-Required, or EIS-Required based on action type, surface disturbance acreage, and extraordinary circumstances flag. Evaluated by the NEPA_CE_Screener Flow after intake save. The authoritative CE code match (which specific 40 CFR / agency regulation row applies) is determined by the downstream CE_Screening_Rules__mdt lookup.
dataSource: CE_Screening_Rules__mdt; 40 CFR Part 1501; CEQ NEPA Regulations (2022); agency-specific NEPA handbooks
regulatory_basis: 40 CFR 1501.4 (Categorical Exclusions); 40 CFR 1501.5 (Environmental Assessments); 40 CFR 1501.11 (Environmental Impact Statements)

inputs.description: Standardized action type from intake (e.g., SurfaceDisturbance, RightOfWay, Maintenance, Administrative)
Physical surface disturbance footprint in acres
Boolean; true if any of the 6 EC triggers are present (critical habitat, EJ, floodplain, wetland, cultural resource, tribal)

outputs.description: Preliminary NEPA pathway: CE-Recommended, EA-Required, or EIS-Required
Screening confidence tier: High, Medium, Low
Plain-language explanation of the routing decision with regulatory citation

outcome.ClassificationBasis: Surface disturbance exceeds 640 acres. EIS typically required at this scale per agency-specific NEPA handbooks and 40 CFR 1501.11. Verify with lead agency NEPA coordinator.

rows.description: Mid-scale surface disturbance with extraordinary circumstances — EA floor

outcome.ClassificationBasis: Surface disturbance exceeds 5-acre CE threshold and extraordinary circumstances are present. CE does not apply per 40 CFR 1501.4(b). EA review required.

rows.description: Mid-scale surface disturbance — EA threshold by acreage

outcome.ClassificationBasis: Surface disturbance exceeds 5-acre CE threshold (40 CFR 1501.4). EA review likely required. NEPA coordinator will verify applicable agency CE code.

rows.description: Small surface disturbance with extraordinary circumstances — CE blocked

outcome.ClassificationBasis: Extraordinary circumstances present — CE may not apply per 40 CFR 1501.4(b) even at small acreage. EA review recommended. NEPA coordinator will evaluate specific EC triggers.

rows.description: Small surface disturbance, no extraordinary circumstances — CE candidate

outcome.ClassificationBasis: Action type and acreage appear within CE thresholds. NEPA coordinator will verify the applicable CE code against agency-specific regulations. Pre-screening result is not a final determination.

rows.description: Administrative or maintenance action — CE candidate regardless of acreage

outcome.ClassificationBasis: Administrative, maintenance, and monitoring actions routinely qualify for CE under most agency NEPA handbooks absent extraordinary circumstances. NEPA coordinator will confirm applicable CE code.

rows.description: Right-of-way or linear project — EA threshold applies by route miles

outcome.ClassificationBasis: Linear projects exceeding 10 acres of disturbance typically require EA-level review. Right-of-way grants and linear facilities carry additional co-permit requirements (CWA 404, ESA Section 7) that elevate review complexity.

notes: This matrix represents DM1 only. The full CE Screener BRE includes 8 Decision Matrices covering action type routing, sector-specific thresholds, agency-specific CE catalog matching, EC trigger evaluation (6 triggers), GIS proximity result integration, and CE code confidence scoring.

The 5-acre threshold used in R002/R003/R004/R005 is representative; actual thresholds vary by agency. BLM uses 5 acres for placer mining; USFS uses different thresholds for timber activities. The downstream CE_Screening_Rules__mdt lookup applies the agency-specific threshold after routing.
Row evaluation is ordered: R001 → R007. First matching row wins. This matrix is invoked after GIS proximity checks complete (Step 6 of OmniScript CE Intake Wizard).
All 2,105 CE authority records are stored in CE_Screening_Rules__mdt. The full dataset is published under /force-app/main/default/customMetadata/ in the project repository.
